headline: Convert the LA window into an inbound funnel — pitch The Town, close two awards, launch The Vertical View.
whatChanged: First brief on the new Claude-Code-routine contract (previous Anthropic-SDK agent pipeline was failing every run). RSS sweep was unavailable in this verification environment (12/12 sources blocked at the network layer) — the next scheduled run in a network-enabled environment will produce the first full intelligence-driven brief. Despite that, the existing seed state surfaces real, time-bounded actions: the StreamTV Awards submission deadline is Jun 15 (fitScore 95, urgent); the Marketing Excellence Awards SG deadline is Jun 5 (13 days); the LA meetings trip starts Jun 1 with no public-narrative scaffolding around it yet; the highest-priority single media pitch (Matthew Belloni / The Town) has never been sent. Tim's documented weekly focus already names the right four moves — this brief sharpens them into specific touchpoints.

topOpportunities.title: Pitch Matthew Belloni / The Town
topOpportunities.why: Belloni already covered DramaBox on The Town. Tim is the B2B distribution counterpart Hollywood will need next, and is the only English-language operator with the receipts (1,700+ titles, 10+ markets, USD 1M+ B2B revenue). One outreach, two surfaces — Puck feature + The Town episode.

topOpportunities.title: Close StreamTV Awards submission — Content Partnerships Executive of the Year
topOpportunities.why: Category title literally matches Tim's role. Deadline Jun 15 (23 days). fitScore 95. Submission writes itself from the existing receipts: 1,700+ titles, USD 1M+ B2B revenue in 6 months, Microdrama-in-a-Box.

topOpportunities.title: Close Cannes Lions Innovation / Creative Business Transformation entry
topOpportunities.why: Listed in Tim's documented weekly focus. Microdrama-in-a-Box is a credible Innovation Lion case study. Fee USD 1,200 — modest at the brand-equity payoff. Status is still 'identified' — must move this week.

topOpportunities.title: Pre-LA narrative sequencing on LinkedIn
topOpportunities.why: LA trip Jun 1-4 is currently a private calendar item. Three posts in the next 9 days (pre-trip questions, daily-on-the-ground, post-trip distillation) turn the trip into a public asset that produces inbound for COL and surfaces Tim's frame in advance of meetings.
topOpportunities.source: data/state/speaking.json — seed-la-meetings-trip; weekly focus 'Build LA trip momentum'

topOpportunities.title: Launch The Vertical View newsletter (issue #1 in 2 weeks)
topOpportunities.why: Quarter goal explicitly. Owned channel converts Tim from interview subject to source-of-record. First-issue topic ready: Australia 20% payment rate. Cadence (every other Sunday) is sustainable. Three issues pre-written before launch makes 'started strong then died' risk manageable.
topOpportunities.source: data/state/settings.json campaignGoals.thisQuarter; speaking.json — Cairns Crocodiles Australia data reveal

topPeople.name: Matthew Belloni
topPeople.whyMatters: Single-pitch unlocks Puck feature + The Town episode. Already covered DramaBox — Tim is the B2B counterpart.
topPeople.timRelevance: Establishes Tim in US tier-1 entertainment-business press. Belloni's audience IS the audience Tim wants.
topPeople.colRelevance: COL's B2B narrative (Microdrama-in-a-Box, distribution rails) lands harder on The Town than any Asian trade outlet.
topPeople.bestApproach: Cold email to matthew@puck.news. Subject: 'The B2B counterpart to your DramaBox story.' Body: 3 numbers (1,700 titles, USD 1M B2B, 238M-day), 1 frame (Vertical 2.0), 1 ask (15-min call before LA trip).
topPeople.publicMove: None yet. Don't comment on Belloni publicly until private pitch lands — wrong sequence.
topPeople.privateMove: Stuart to draft the pitch by Mon May 25, send by Tue May 26.
topPeople.risk: Generic founder-pitch email gets binned. Mitigation: lead with the 238M-day number, not with COL's name.

topPeople.name: Maria Rua Aguete
topPeople.whyMatters: Co-panellist at NEM Dubrovnik (Jun 8). Tim's most-cited data anchor (USD 11B/22B sizing). Already a warm relationship.
topPeople.timRelevance: Sync ahead of the Dubrovnik panel — agree which numbers each of them owns on stage to avoid stepping on each other.
topPeople.colRelevance: Omdia data is the citation that makes COL-narrative claims credible to outside-the-bubble audiences.
topPeople.bestApproach: WhatsApp / LinkedIn DM by end of next week. Tone: warm peer, not formal.
topPeople.privateMove: Send Tim's Cairns data points before she finalises her NEM deck — gives her a fresh datapoint to credit Tim with on stage.

topPeople.name: Connie Chan
topPeople.whyMatters: Consumer + China crossover thesis. LA trip is the natural window for an in-person.
topPeople.timRelevance: Warm intro into a16z podcast (consumer / China / mobile-first) and West Coast investor network.
topPeople.colRelevance: Investor narrative validation. Future cap-stack signal.
topPeople.bestApproach: Warm intro request — identify a mutual (likely via Singapore / Stanford / earlier a16z portfolio).
topPeople.privateMove: Stuart to identify the warm intro vector and brief Tim by Wed May 27 so he can reach out before LA wheels-up.
topPeople.risk: Cold-in attempt without a warm path gets ignored.

topPeople.name: Janine Stein
topPeople.whyMatters: Most consistent media amplifier; broke the CMO appointment. Give her the next exclusive (newsletter launch + an Australia-20% data feature).
topPeople.timRelevance: Maintains relationship cadence between MIP London / MIPCOM. Keeps Tim in regular APAC trade coverage.
topPeople.colRelevance: ContentAsia's audience IS the Asian content-business decision-maker layer COL wants to be visible to.
topPeople.bestApproach: Email + LinkedIn DM with The Vertical View launch ahead of public announcement; offer the Australia 20% data as an accompanying feature.
topPeople.privateMove: Reach out within 48 hours of newsletter launch decision being locked.

topPeople.name: Lisa Cam
topPeople.whyMatters: Wrote the SCMP full-page feature Tim called his favourite. Natural follow-up on the newsletter launch — Chinese-language Western press is currently under-served.
topPeople.timRelevance: Bridges Tim's narrative into Chinese-language Western media. Distinct audience from the Western trade outlets.
topPeople.colRelevance: COL's investor base reads SCMP. Maintains visibility into Hong Kong / PRC-adjacent commercial circles.
topPeople.bestApproach: Personal email — thank her for the prior piece, share Vertical View launch as a 'thought you'd find this interesting', no formal pitch.
topPeople.privateMove: Send personal note on newsletter launch day.

bestMediaAngle.angle: B2B distribution as the next phase of vertical drama — Tim has the rails Hollywood is about to need. Frame: "You covered DramaBox (consumer). The interesting story now is who supplies the picks and shovels. That's COL."
bestMediaAngle.why: Belloni's audience is US entertainment-business decision-makers. He's already framed the DramaBox story for them; the B2B counterpart is the natural follow-on. One email can land Puck feature + The Town episode + downstream tier-1 attention.
bestMediaAngle.evidence: Belloni's prior DramaBox coverage (Puck / The Town). Tim's documented commercial proof (1,700+ titles, USD 1M+ B2B revenue, 238M-day, 77-min sessions). Pitch contact already in data/state/media.json.

bestLinkedInAngle.hook: Nine days. LA. Five questions I'm going to ask everyone.
bestLinkedInAngle.angle: Pre-LA agenda-setting post. Operator coming to ask questions, not pitch. Numbered list (Tim's natural format). Closes with an inbound ask.
bestLinkedInAngle.why: Turns the LA trip from a private calendar item into a public asset before Tim lands. Surfaces inbound from people who want to meet. Sets Tim's frame for how journalists / agencies / studios read his LA meetings in retrospect. Sounds like Tim — operator, not influencer.

bestCommentOpportunity.targetAuthor: NONE — radar walk produced no verified target post this run.
bestCommentOpportunity.suggestedComment: Hold. The opportunity radar's 'comment' category requires a verified live post by a named person, with a comment that lands without inventing context. Without the RSS sweep + manual LinkedIn check, picking a target this run risks fabrication. Re-run when intelligence sweep is available.
bestCommentOpportunity.why: Honest deferral. CLAUDE.md guardrail: never imply a relationship / interest that doesn't exist.
bestCommentOpportunity.risk: Manufactured comments degrade Tim's voice. Skipping a category this week is the right call.

bestRelationshipMove.name: Matthew Belloni
bestRelationshipMove.whyMatters: Single-pitch, two-surface upside (Puck + The Town). Highest leverage per minute spent of any move in this brief.
bestRelationshipMove.timRelevance: Establishes Tim in US tier-1 entertainment-business press in one move.
bestRelationshipMove.colRelevance: B2B narrative lands harder here than anywhere else.
bestRelationshipMove.bestApproach: Cold email matthew@puck.news. Subject: 'The B2B counterpart to your DramaBox story.' 3 numbers, 1 frame, 1 ask. 15-min call before LA trip.
bestRelationshipMove.privateMove: Stuart to draft Mon May 25, send Tue May 26. Tim to be ready for a 15-min call any time Jun 1-4 in LA.
bestRelationshipMove.risk: Generic founder-pitch energy. Mitigation: lead with the 238M-day stat, not with COL's name.

bestColOpportunity.title: Use the LA trip to land one US studio/streamer Microdrama-in-a-Box conversation
bestColOpportunity.why: Microdrama-in-a-Box is the cleanest B2B asset COL has — turnkey SaaS that lets any platform run a vertical service in 30 days. The LA trip is the only confirmed in-person window before MIPCOM in October. One serious studio/streamer conversation justifies the trip commercially even if nothing else lands.
bestColOpportunity.commercialUpside: Any one of: a white-label deal (recurring SaaS revenue), a co-investment in a vertical originals slate, or a strategic POC with a major streamer. Each is a multi-million-dollar opportunity and a tier-1 PR moment if it lands.
bestColOpportunity.nextStep: Stuart + Tim build the LA target list by Wed May 27. Goal: 3 confirmed meetings with C-suite or SVP-level decision-makers at major US studios/streamers. Pre-trip LinkedIn post lands Sun May 31 to set context.

risingTrend.trend: Brand advertisers entering vertical drama as a media line item, not as a one-off stunt.
risingTrend.evidence: P&G, Unilever, Crocs, Shopee already named in Tim's frame (knowledge/STRATEGIC_PROFILE.md). The Cairns Crocodiles audience was Australia's marketing community — confirming the brand-side appetite is moving from curious to budgeted.
risingTrend.implication: Tim's positioning needs to drift from 'microdrama operator' to 'commercial bridge between brand CMOs and vertical infrastructure'. Action: at least one upcoming content piece should be aimed squarely at brand CMOs, not at peers in the microdrama industry.

thingToIgnore.item: Hollywood "vertical pivot" think-pieces that don't include actual data.
thingToIgnore.why: Tim's whole frame is 'we're not in the business of saving Hollywood'. Commenting on opinion-only think-pieces makes Tim sound like he's defending against a frame that doesn't apply. The discipline is: only engage when there's a number to anchor on.

reputationRisk.risk: Post-CMO promotion (May 2026), Tim's content risks sounding triumphalist if Stuart isn't watching. The voice tilts naturally toward 'look at the milestone' rather than 'here's what we're solving next'.
reputationRisk.mitigation: Voice rule reinforced: open with a number or a one-line claim, never with 'I'm excited to share'. Stuart to flag and rewrite any draft that opens on Tim's title rather than on the work.

suggestedAction.action: By end of Tue May 26: (1) draft + send Belloni pitch, (2) confirm StreamTV Awards submission owner, (3) confirm Cannes Lions submission owner, (4) green-light Vertical View newsletter cadence and pre-write issues #1-3, (5) start the LA target list with Tim. By Sat May 30: post #1 of the LA pre-trip series goes live.
